FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: An Offer You Can't Refuse
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: A lavishly dressed trader has arrived at your settlement with an entourage of guards and pack animals loaded with silver. He introduces himself as a "facilitator of opportunity" and proposes a long-term exclusive trade arrangement. The terms seem almost too good: premium prices for your goods, guaranteed buyers, no questions asked. Your instincts say there's a catch.

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Accept the deal. Silver is silver, and his is as good as anyone's.
Counter-offer. If he wants exclusivity, he pays a premium for it.
Decline. We don't need outsiders controlling our trade.
Our trade network gives us leverage. We'll deal, but as equal partners.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Opportunity Refused
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The merchant takes his rejection with a thin smile and departs with his caravan. Settlers who caught wind of the offer grumble about the lost opportunity. They'll get over it.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Sweet Deal
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The merchant's operation moves in quickly. Within days, his agents are handling all trade coming through the settlement. Prices are excellent — noticeably better than before. But local merchants are being squeezed out, and the settler rumor mill churns with speculation about where his goods really come from.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Renegotiated Terms
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your counter-offer impressed the merchant. The revised agreement protects local businesses while still granting him access to your markets. The arrangement is profitable without being predatory, and your settlers appreciate that their leaders drove a hard bargain.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Insulted Merchant
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The merchant takes your counter-offer as an insult. He packs up and leaves — loudly. Worse, he makes good on his parting threat to badmouth your settlement to every caravan on the rim. Trade dries up for a season as merchants give your markets a wide berth.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Partnership of Equals
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your trade expertise forces the merchant to deal honestly. The resulting partnership is quite equitable: he gains access to your markets, you gain access to his supply chain, and both sides profit fairly. Your settlers are proud to see the {FACTION_TITLE}'s commercial strength on display.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: The Merchant's True Colors
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc:
Disturbing reports are filtering in. The merchant's agents have been caught smuggling contraband through your settlement, using your trade routes as cover. And to make matters worse, local shopkeepers complain they're being systematically undercut — the merchant sells goods below cost to drive them out of business.
His "sweet deal" is starting to look like a takeover.

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Confront him directly. This ends now.
Investigate secretly. Let's find out exactly what he's been doing before we act.
Look the other way. The silver keeps flowing and that's what matters.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Growing Partnership
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The partnership has been a success. The merchant has proven himself trustworthy, and both sides have profited handsomely. He approaches you with a new proposal: expand the arrangement to cover more of your settlements. Alternatively, you could keep the partnership focused where it is.

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Expand the partnership across more settlements. A good thing shouldn't be limited.
Keep the arrangement to this settlement. No need to spread the risk.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Trade Network Expanded
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The merchant's network now operates across multiple settlements, creating a reliable trade backbone for your {FACTION_TITLE}.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Focused Partnership
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The partnership remains concentrated in a single settlement, where it continues to thrive. The merchant respects your caution and dedicates his full attention to making this one arrangement as profitable as possible.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: The Bribe
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: You confront the merchant in his warehouse. He doesn't even try to deny it — he simply opens a chest overflowing with silver and slides it across the table. "Everyone has a price," he says with a knowing smile. "Think of it as a consulting fee for your continued... discretion."

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Take the silver. Everyone does have a price, and his is reasonable.
Refuse. Seize his goods and throw him out of the settlement.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Pocketed Silver
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: You take the merchant's silver and look the other way. The smuggling continues, but his "consulting fees" are generous. Somewhere in the back of your mind, you know this will catch up with you — the tax base erodes as the merchant's parallel economy thrives in the shadows.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Merchant Expelled
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Guards escort the merchant and his people out of the settlement under the cheers of local shopkeepers. The smuggling network is dismantled, legitimate trade resumes, and your settlers are proud that their leaders couldn't be bought. It's a good day for the {FACTION_TITLE}.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Evidence Found
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your investigators have done excellent work. They've documented everything: the smuggling routes, the bribed customs officials, the warehouses full of contraband, and the merchant's personal ledger detailing every illegal transaction. You have him dead to rights. The question is what to do with this leverage.

FactionColonies.FCOptionDef.label: Arrest him and seize his goods. Justice must be public and decisive.
Blackmail him. He works for us now — on our terms, with our cut.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Merchant Arrested
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The merchant is dragged before the settlement in chains, his warehouses emptied, his agents scattered. The seized goods are distributed among the settlers, and the public trial sends a clear message: the {FACTION_TITLE} protects its own. Loyalty soars.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Merchant Under Thumb
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: The merchant has no choice but to comply. His trade network now operates under your direction, funneling premium profits into your treasury. He does his work with a forced smile, knowing that one wrong move means the evidence goes public. The arrangement is lucrative, but your settlers sense something rotten about the whole affair.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Spooked
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc: Your investigators were careless. The merchant caught wind of the probe and vanished overnight, along with his agents, his goods, and a disturbing amount of your settlement's trade contacts. The local economy stumbles without the established trade routes he controlled.

FactionColonies.FCEventDef.label: Monopoly
FactionColonies.FCEventDef.desc:
By looking the other way, you allowed the merchant to consolidate total control over local trade. Every business in the settlement either works for him or is out of business. The silver flows like water — into his pockets first, then a generous cut to you.
But the citzens are furious. Local artisans have been ruined, prices are whatever the merchant says they are, and the settlement feels less like a community and more like a company town.
